content: Calculate the chi-square statistic to assess the association between histological types and the presence of CDH1 gene mutations in female BRCA patients from the PanCancer Atlas, excluding categories with marginal totals less than or equal to 10, and only focusing on patients with known histological types and consider only reliable mutation entries.

message.content: Calculate the chi-square statistic to assess the association between histological types and the presence of CDH1 gene mutations in female BRCA patients from the PanCancer Atlas, excluding categories with marginal totals less than or equal to 10, and only focusing on patients with known histological types and consider only reliable mutation entries.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Chi-square analysis of CDH1 mutations and histology in BRCA patients

input.command: psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U user -d pancancer_clinical -c "SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'clinical_info' AND (column_name ILIKE '%gender%' OR column_name ILIKE '%sex%' OR column_name ILIKE '%acronym%' OR column_name ILIKE '%bcr%' OR column_name ILIKE '%barcode%' OR column_name ILIKE '%cancer%type%');"
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U user -d pancancer_clinical -c "SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'clinical_info' ORDER BY column_name;" | head -200

wc -l /tmp/brca_female.csv
head -3 /tmp/brca_female.csv

input.command:
duckdb '/workspace/DataAgentBench/query_PANCANCER_ATLAS/query_dataset/pancancer_molecular.db' -readonly -csv "SELECT DISTINCT ParticipantBarcode FROM Mutation_Data WHERE Hugo_Symbol='CDH1' AND FILTER='PASS';" > /tmp/cdh1_mut.csv
wc -l /tmp/cdh1_mut.csv

cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/chi.py
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import chi2_contingency

clin = pd.read_csv('/tmp/brca_female.csv')
mut = pd.read_csv('/tmp/cdh1_mut.csv')

# Dedup clinical in case
clin = clin.drop_duplicates(subset=['barcode'])
print("Unique female BRCA patients with known histological type:", len(clin))

mut_set = set(mut['ParticipantBarcode'])
clin['CDH1_mut'] = clin['barcode'].isin(mut_set).map({True: 'Mutated', False: 'Not Mutated'})

ct = pd.crosstab(clin['histological_type'], clin['CDH1_mut'])
print("\nFull contingency table:")
print(ct)

# Marginal totals (row totals)
row_totals = ct.sum(axis=1)
print("\nRow totals:")
print(row_totals)

# Keep rows with totals > 10
keep_rows = row_totals[row_totals > 10].index
ct_filtered = ct.loc[keep_rows]
print("\nFiltered contingency table (row totals > 10):")
print(ct_filtered)
print("\nRow totals of filtered:")
print(ct_filtered.sum(axis=1))
print("\nColumn totals of filtered:")
print(ct_filtered.sum(axis=0))

# Chi-square
chi2, p, dof, exp = chi2_contingency(ct_filtered.values)
print(f"\nChi-square statistic: {chi2:.4f}")
print(f"p-value: {p:.6e}")
print(f"Degrees of freedom: {dof}")
print("\nExpected frequencies:")
print(pd.DataFrame(exp, index=ct_filtered.index, columns=ct_filtered.columns))
EOF
python3 /tmp/chi.py
